data.title: SSR Mining targets up to $535M in cash flow and $300M share buyback amid robust mine performance and new project milestones
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: SSR Mining Inc. (SSRM:CA) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Executive Chairman Rodney Antal highlighted, "We closed 2025 on a high note, delivering full year production above the midpoint of our guidance range and generated more than $100 million in free cash flow in the fourth quarter." He added, "we finished the year with $535 million in cash and more than $1 billion in liquidity," and announced a Board-approved share buyback of up to $300 million, stating, "we are pleased to announce that our Board has approved a share buyback of up to $300 million." Antal also called attention to strong results from the Cripple Creek and Victor mine and Puna operations, both exceeding full year guidance and delivering exceptional free cash flow. He described the Puna mine as achieving production guidance for the third consecutive year and setting records for tonnes processed.
  • Antal noted the release of two technical report summaries, including Cripple Creek and Victor's 12-year life-of-mine plan with an $824 million NPV, and Hod Maden's $1.7 billion NPV and 39% IRR. He emphasized, "Hod Maden is a unique project with significant scale, best-in-class grades and first quartile all-in sustaining costs that position the asset to deliver compelling free cash flow in the future."
  • CFO Michael Sparks stated, "In 2026, we expect to produce between 450,000 and 535,000 gold equivalent ounces... All-in sustaining costs are expected to range between $2,360 and $2,440 per ounce or $2,180 to $2,260 per ounce, excluding the impact of care and maintenance costs at Çöpler." Sparks added, "Total gross spend is expected to total $150 million in 2026, driven mainly by capital investments in leach pad expansions at both Marigold and CC&V as well as continued exploration and resource development spend globally."
  • William MacNevin, EVP Operations & Sustainability, reported, "We closed 2025 with 11 million ounces of gold equivalent mineral reserves, a testament to the scale and longevity of our diversified operating platform. Reserves were up nearly 40% year-over-year, driven largely by the incorporation of CC&V and Hod Maden into our consolidated totals."

Outlook

  • SSR Mining projects 2026 production between 450,000 and 535,000 gold equivalent ounces from Marigold, CC&V, Seabee, and Puna, with all-in sustaining costs expected between $2,360 and $2,440 per ounce. Excluding Çöpler, AISC is guided at $2,180 to $2,260 per ounce. Cash care and maintenance costs at Çöpler are guided at $20 million to $25 million per quarter. Total gross spend is expected at $150 million, including leach pad expansions and exploration. CapEx at Hod Maden is set at up to $15 million per month pending a construction decision. Management expects material free cash flow generation to continue in 2026.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, guidance has shifted from expecting production at the lower half of the range to explicitly aiming for higher production and continued free cash flow strength.

Financial Results

  • In Q4 2025, SSR Mining produced 120,000 gold equivalent ounces at an AISC of $2,250 per ounce, or $202 per ounce excluding Çöpler costs. Quarterly sales reached 117,000 gold equivalent ounces with an average realized gold price of $4,142 per ounce. Net income attributable to shareholders was $181 million, or $0.84 per diluted share; adjusted net income was $190 million, or $0.88 per diluted share.
  • Full year production totaled 447,000 gold equivalent ounces, above the midpoint of guidance. Full-year AISC, excluding Çöpler, was $1,923 per ounce. Free cash flow reached $106 million in Q4 and $252 million for the full year; excluding working capital, full year free cash flow exceeded $400 million. Year-end cash stood at $535 million with liquidity over $1 billion.

Q&A

  • George Eadie, UBS: Asked about Marigold stacking grades and conservatism in guidance. William MacNevin responded that recent technical work and updated blending plans are now integrated, stating, "we believe that guidance is a good indication of what we'll deliver this year."
  • Eadie also probed on five-year production references, with Antal clarifying that blending requirements and recent work would be incorporated into a new technical report with updated profiles.
  • Eadie, UBS: Questioned Puna's production extension. MacNevin explained that ongoing work at Chinchillas, Molina, and Cortaderas supports potential extension, noting, "silver prices more than support that."
  • Cosmos Chiu, CIBC: Inquired about Hod Maden's construction decision timeline. Antal said work continues on site and a decision will follow ongoing partner reviews, but declined to set a specific timeline.
  • Chiu, CIBC: Noted Puna's 2026 production guidance is lower than previous studies. MacNevin attributed this to timing changes, with some ounces deferred into later years.
  • Chiu, CIBC: Asked about durable vs nondurable ore at Marigold. MacNevin detailed blending strategies to optimize recovery.
  • Ovais Habib, Scotiabank: Asked if Marigold's blending issue is resolved. Antal said, "it's not a one-off. It's going to be a future feature for Marigold."
  • Don DeMarco, National Bank: Queried Hod Maden spending ahead of construction. Antal clarified that ongoing early site work justifies current spending levels.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts pressed for details on production guidance, project timelines, blending strategies, and potential mine life extensions, with a tone that was neutral to slightly positive and focused on technical clarification and timing.
  • Management maintained a confident and transparent tone in prepared remarks and Q&A, frequently referencing technical work, guidance, and ongoing project milestones. Phrases such as "we believe that guidance is a good indication of what we'll deliver" and "the future is pretty bright for Puna" reflected optimism.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analysts' tone shifted from cautious to more constructive, with management's confidence rising as operational issues were addressed and new milestones achieved.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for 2026 is more optimistic, with higher production targets and explicit share buyback plans, compared to a more cautious outlook in Q3 2025.
  • Strategic focus has evolved from resolving operational challenges and preparing technical reports to executing on growth projects, initiating share buybacks, and extending mine lives.
  • Analysts in Q4 focused on technical and operational execution, while in Q3 they were more concerned with cost pressures, project timelines, and remediation at Çöpler.
  • Management confidence increased, moving from addressing operational obstacles to highlighting growth, financial strength, and portfolio expansion.

Risks and Concerns

  • Blending issues at Marigold are expected to be a recurring challenge, with management integrating technical solutions and scheduling updates to address recovery performance.
  • Çöpler remains on care and maintenance with ongoing government discussions and no material operational progress since the last quarter.
  • Puna's production guidance for 2026 is slightly lower due to timing, though management expects production to remain at higher levels for longer into future years.
  • Hod Maden's construction decision is pending partner reviews, though early site works and civil projects are ongoing.

Final Takeaway

SSR Mining concluded 2025 in a strong financial position, marked by robust free cash flow, a $300 million share buyback authorization, and substantial progress across key projects such as Cripple Creek & Victor, Puna, and Hod Maden. The company projects continued production growth and free cash flow generation in 2026, underpinned by technical advancements, mine life extensions, and disciplined capital allocation. Management remains focused on delivering operational consistency, advancing development pipelines, and optimizing shareholder returns through both organic growth and capital management initiatives.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Beta Bionics outlines $130M–$135M 2026 revenue target while expanding sales force and pharmacy channel
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Beta Bionics, Inc. (BBNX) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Sean Saint, President and CEO, reflected on the company's first full year as a public entity, highlighting, "Close to 20,000 new users adopted our technology in 2025, more than doubling our installed base entering the year, which now stands about 35,000 total users that have adopted the iLet since launch." He emphasized the productivity of the sales force, the expansion of pharmacy channel access, and the company's gross margin strength, stating, "We also believe our gross margin profile is already the strongest in the durable pump space... especially considering the success that we've seen in the pharmacy channel, which had a short-term dilutive effect on gross margin in 2025."
  • Saint highlighted the launch of the Mint patch pump program and key clinical milestones in the bihormonal system, noting the completion of first clinical trials for their glucagon asset and a feasibility trial for the bihormonal system.
  • Regarding the regulatory environment, Saint addressed the FDA warning letter related to their Irvine facility, explaining, "We take the FDA's observations very seriously... I remain proud of the incredible work our teams are doing to address the agency's concerns and confident in our ability to resolve them."
  • CFO Stephen Feider provided additional details: "Our Q4 revenue, pharmacy mix and gross margin results exceeded our guidance across the board."
  • Feider indicated that 69% of Q4 new patient starts came from people previously using multiple daily injections, reinforcing market expansion efforts.

Outlook

  • Feider stated, "We expect to generate $130 million to $135 million of revenue in 2026. On our channel mix, we expect 36% to 38% of our new patient starts to be reimbursed through the pharmacy channel. Lastly, we expect gross margin to be between 55.5% and 57.5%."
  • Management expects sales force expansion by at least 20 new territories in 2026 and ongoing cost discipline, while guiding that OpEx and CapEx will increase as a percentage of revenue.
  • Feider noted, "We expect Q1 to decline sequentially from Q4 2025," citing typical seasonality and the lack of comparable product launches compared to the prior year.
  • Management will no longer provide exact quarterly new patient starts, instead offering quarterly revenue by product and channel, and trend-based commentary.

Financial Results

  • Beta Bionics reported $100.3 million in net sales for full year 2025, representing 54% year-over-year growth.
  • Q4 net sales were $32.1 million, up 57% year-over-year, driven by 5,592 new patient starts, retention in the pharmacy channel, and favorable stocking revenue.
  • Gross margin for Q4 reached 59%, a 179 basis point increase year-over-year. Full year gross margin was 55.4%.
  • Total operating expenses in Q4 were $35.1 million, up 42% from the prior year, with investments in sales, R&D, and public company operations.
  • As of December 31, 2025, the company reported $265 million in cash, cash equivalents, and investments.

Q&A

  • Philip Coover, Goldman Sachs: Asked about conservatism in 2026 guidance and new patient starts embedded in the forecast. Feider replied, "I don't want to call the guidance for 2026 conservative... we do, of course, have confidence in hitting the guidance that we've communicated."
  • Coover: Inquired about gross margin guidance and pharmacy conversion rates. Feider explained, "In the event that we massively outperformed our guidance in 2026 in terms of pharmacy new patient starts. That... will create a short-term drag on gross margin and hence, we're guiding gross margin where we have."
  • Michael Polark, Wolfe Research: Asked about Q4 market share and sales territory expansion. Feider stated, "The guidance is at least 20 territories, will be expanding by in 2026, and there will be a large expansion in the first half of the year."
  • Mathew Blackman, TD Cowen: Questioned revenue seasonality and territory coverage. Feider confirmed, "We are probably covering actually more than 33% to 50% of the entire country," and discussed the potential impact of additional territories.
  • Jonathan Block, Stifel: Asked about the rationale for 20 new reps and type 2 diabetes adoption. Feider said, "Expansion is absolutely the intention of the company... but I also did say that we're going to expand by at least 20." Saint added, "We always believe in being deliberate... Beta Bionics is playing the long game here."
  • Matthew O'Brien, Piper Sandler: Inquired about the revenue guide and gross margin step down. Feider responded, "We're just setting a guide that we have confidence in and we feel good about."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts pressed for clarity on guidance conservatism, pharmacy channel impacts, and sales force expansion, showing a neutral to slightly skeptical tone, especially on the sustainability of growth and margin assumptions.
  • Management maintained a confident but measured tone in both prepared remarks and Q&A, using phrases such as "we have confidence in hitting the guidance" and emphasizing deliberate expansion, but also repeatedly qualified responses to avoid overcommitment.
  • Relative to the previous quarter, both analysts and management continued with a disciplined and fact-based approach, but current sentiment reflected increased scrutiny on growth sustainability and guidance methodology.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q4 revenue and gross margin both increased versus Q3, with Q4 net sales at $32.1 million (vs. $27.3 million in Q3) and gross margin at 59% (vs. 55.5% in Q3).
  • Full year 2026 revenue guidance was introduced at $130 million to $135 million, compared to raised 2025 guidance in Q3 that projected at least $96.5 million.
  • Management shifted to less granular disclosure on new patient starts, reflecting greater alignment with industry practices.
  • Analysts' focus evolved from growth drivers and pipeline progress in Q3 to deeper questions on guidance confidence, pharmacy channel mix, and seasonality impacts in Q4.
  • The tone shifted to cautious optimism, with management reiterating cost discipline amid higher OpEx and CapEx expectations for 2026.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management addressed the FDA warning letter, outlining remediation steps for complaint handling, MDR definitions, CAPA processes, and corrections and removals procedures.
  • Feider noted variability in pharmacy mix and sales force expansion as key variables that could impact revenue and gross margin.
  • Seasonality and the lack of new product launches in late 2025 were cited as headwinds for Q1 2026.
  • Analysts raised concerns regarding potential competitive impacts in the pharmacy channel, attrition, and the pace of sales force expansion.

Final Takeaway

Beta Bionics closed its first full year as a public company with robust revenue growth, expanding its user base and pharmacy channel presence, and achieving record gross margins. Management projects $130 million to $135 million in revenue for 2026, with continued focus on expanding sales territories, advancing the Mint and bihormonal pump programs, and executing on pharmacy channel strategy. Efforts to address regulatory findings are underway, with confidence expressed in remediation and ongoing transparency. The company positions itself for sustained growth and increased market share while remaining attentive to operational risks, regulatory compliance, and evolving industry disclosure practices.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Fiverr International reports mixed Q4 results; initiates Q1 and FY26 outlook
data.description:
• Fiverr International press release (FVRR): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.86 beats by $0.12.
  • Revenue of $107.2M (+3.4% Y/Y) misses by $1.78M.
  • Marketplace revenue in the fourth quarter of 2025 was $71.5 million, compared to $73.5 million in the fourth quarter of 2024, a decline of 2.7% year over year.
  • Annual active buyers as of December 31, 2025, were 3.1 million, compared to 3.6 million as of December 31, 2024, a decline of 13.6% year over year.
  • Annual spend per buyer as of December 31, 2025, reached $342, compared to $302 as of December 31, 2024, an increase of 13.3% year over year.
  • Marketplace take rate1 for the twelve months period ended December 31, 2025, was 27.7%, an increase of 10 basis points from 27.6% for the twelve months period ended December 31, 2024.
  • Services revenue in the fourth quarter of 2025 was $35.6 million, compared to $30.2 million in the fourth quarter of 2024, an increase of 18.2% year over year.
  • Adjusted EBITDA1 in the fourth quarter of 2025 was $26.5 million, compared to $20.7 million in the fourth quarter of 2024.
  • Free cash flow1 in the fourth quarter of 2025 was $21.8 million, compared to $29.6 million in the fourth quarter of 2024, a decrease of 26.5% year over year.
  • Net cash provided by operating activities in the fourth quarter of 2025 was $21.9 million, compared to $30.0 million in the fourth quarter of 2024, a decrease of 27.2% year over year.

  • Financial Outlook

    For Q1'26 and full-year 2026 guidance

data.title: TJ Maxx and JPMorgan Chase renew 68,120-sq-ft retail leases with Empire State Realty Trust
data.description:
• Empire State Realty Trust (ESRT) Tuesday said that it signed long-term renewal leases with TJ Maxx and JP Morgan Chase Bank (JPM) for retail spaces for 46,437 and 21,683 square feet, respectively.
  • TJ Maxx signed a renewal lease for its 46,437 square foot space at 250 W. 57th Street in Q4 2025. Richard Skulnik of RIPCO represented TJ Maxx in the lease negotiations.
  • JP Morgan Chase Bank signed a renewal lease for its 21,683 square foot space at One Grand Central Place in Q1 2026. Michael O’Neill of Cushman & Wakefield represented JP Morgan Chase Bank in the lease negotiations.
  • Shares in Empire State Realty Trust (ESRT) rose 3.3% in extended trading to $6.60.

data.title: Western Digital to sell ~$3.2B of Sandisk stock in secondary deal
data.description:
Sandisk (SNDK) has announced the pricing of a secondary offering of 5,821,135 shares currently owned by its former parent, Western Digital (WDC), at $545 per share.

Sandisk is not selling any shares and will not receive any proceeds from the offering.

Before the deal closes, WDC is expected to exchange the shares for certain debt held by affiliates of J.P. Morgan Securities and BofA Securities. Upon the consummation of the debt-for-equity exchange, WDC is expected to deliver the SNDK shares, at the request of the debt-for-equity exchange parties, to J.P. Morgan Securities and BofA Securities, in their capacity as selling stockholders in the offering.

After the exchange, WDC is expected to retain 1,691,884 Sandisk shares, which it plans to dispose of later through additional exchanges or distributions to its shareholders.

SNDK shares fell 2% after-hours on Tuesday.

data.title: Japan's trade deficit beats forecasts; China and Taiwan demand fuels 17% Japan export jump
data.description:
Japan's trade deficit fell significantly to JPY 1,152.7 billion in January 2026, down from JPY 2,741.7 billion a year earlier, and was better than the expected deficit of JPY 2,142.1 billion.

This improvement was driven by a 16.8% increase in exports to JPY 9,187.5 billion, the highest growth rate since November 2022, fueled by strong demand from China and other Asian countries before the Lunar New Year. Notable increases were seen in exports to Hong Kong (73.1%), Taiwan (35.3%), and Vietnam (30.6%), among others, while shipments to the U.S. fell by 5%.

Meanwhile, imports decreased by 2.5% year on year to JPY 10,340.2 billion, contrary to market expectations of a rise of 3% and reversing December’s 5.2% increase, indicating weaker domestic demand and lower energy costs after a previous increase in December.

Separately, Japan announced plans to invest $36 billion in US oil, gas, and critical mineral projects as part of its $550 billion commitment under a trade agreement with US President Donald Trump.

The Reuters Tankan index for Japanese manufacturers rose to +13 in February 2026 from +7 in January, marking the first improvement in three months, driven by firmer machinery orders and a weaker yen. 

The IMF reiterated that it does not target the yen’s level, noting that the currency’s value is determined by market forces. 

The Nikkei 225 Index rose 1.26% to above 57,000, while the broader Topix Index gained 1% to 3,800 on Wednesday, recouping losses from earlier in the week as Japan reported strong export growth. The Japanese yen fell to around 153.5 per dollar on Wednesday, giving back gains from the previous session despite strong trade data.

More on Japan's economy:

data.title: Agnico Eagle Mines buys 662,780 shares of Maple Gold Mines for C$1.62M
data.description:
• Agnico Eagle Mines (AEM) acquired 662,780 common shares of Maple Gold Mines (MGM:CA) (MGMLF) at C$2.45 each, totaling C$1.6M, from sellers involved in Maple’s recent flow-through share offering.
  • Canada-based gold producer Agnico Eagle acquired the common shares as part of its strategy of acquiring strategic positions in prospective opportunities with high geological potential, it said in a statement on Tuesday.

data.title: Asia stocks gain in thin holiday trade as US-Iran nuclear talks boost sentiment; Nikkei surges on US-Japan trade deal
Peter Thiel exits Ethereum treasury firm ETHZilla, filing shows
data.description:
• Peter Thiel has fully exited his stake in Ethereum treasury firm ETHZilla (ETHZ) by the end of 2025, according to a recent filing.
  • ETHZilla shares fell 3% in extended trading, down 28% so far this year.
  • Earlier this month, the company’s wholly owned subsidiary, ETHZilla Aerospace,  launched the Eurus Aero Token I, a tokenized asset allowing investors to gain exposure to leased aircraft engines via tradable digital tokens representing revenue rights.

data.title: Carrefour Non-GAAP EPS of €1.60, revenue of €84.03B
data.description:
• Carrefour press release (CRRFY): FY Non-GAAP EPS of €1.60.
  • Revenue of €84.03B (+0.7% Y/Y).
  • Net Free Cash-Flow: €1,565m in 2025 excluding Italy (€1,305m reported).
  • 5.4% increase in the ordinary dividend to €0.97 per share, compared with €0.92 last year; special dividend of €150m (or €0.21 per share), contingent on the closing of the Carrefour Romania disposal.
  • 2026 OUTLOOK: "The Group enters fiscal year 2026 with confidence. In France, the good performance of the legacy scope and the ramp-up of Cora & Match will contribute to the performance of the year within a market showing positive momentum. In Spain, the good commercial momentum is expected to continue with sustained growth, while in Brazil, the Group anticipates a gradual recovery in volumes. Beyond these fundamentals, the Group will benefit from favorable technical and structural tailwinds, such as the end of Cora & Match integration costs and the ramp up of synergies, as well as the full positive impact of the refinancing of the Brazilian debt. "
